content.text: according to what you've told me before, Mira settled best when **swaddled on her back** as a young baby, and you dropped the swaddle at **5 months**. that lines up well with what's typical — the swaddle mimics the snugness of the womb and dampens the startle reflex that wakes newborns, so most families find it helpful in the early weeks; month 5 is a common transition point because babies start showing signs of rolling, which makes a traditional swaddle unsafe to continue. if you ever want a refresher on how you handled that transition (e.g., one-arm-out approach), just ask and i can walk through what's generally done at that stage.
